title: DocSpec Intent Graph
description: DSTI (Deep Structural and Textual Intent) intent graph — extracted intent signals from source code analysis. This standalone schema defines the full structure for intent graphs produced by DocSpec processors across all supported languages.

artifact.description: Artifact coordinates identifying the source of this intent graph.

methods.description: Array of methods with their extracted intent signals.

crossChannelVerification.description: Results of cross-channel consistency verification across all methods.

gapReport.description: Report identifying methods with missing or incomplete intent channels.

ArtifactCoordinates.description: Maven-style artifact coordinates identifying the project or module.

IntentMethod.description: A method with its fully qualified name and extracted intent signals from DSTI analysis.

qualified.description: Fully qualified method name including class and package (e.g. 'com.example.UserService.findById').

intentSignals.description: Intent signals extracted from this method's source code across all 13 DSTI channels.

IntentSignals.description: Intent signals extracted from a method's source code. Contains data from all 13 DSTI channels plus composite scoring.

nameSemantics.description: Channel 1: Semantics extracted from the method name via verb-object decomposition.

oneOf.description: Simple count of guard clauses detected.

guardClauses.description: Channel 2: Guard clauses detected at method entry points — either a count or structured objects.

branches.description: Channel 3: Conditional branches — either a count or a structured analysis object.

nameBehaviorGap.description: Channel 4: Detected discrepancy between what the method name implies and what the method body actually does.

returnType.description: Channel 5: Analysis of the method's return type and its implications for intent.

dataFlow.description: Channel 6: Data flow analysis — what the method reads from and writes to.

loopProperties.description: Channel 7: Loop and iteration analysis including stream operations.

errorHandling.description: Channel 8: Error handling patterns detected in the method body.

oneOf.description: Simple list of constant names referenced.

constants.description: Channel 9: Constants referenced in the method — either simple names or structured objects.

nullChecks.description: Channel 10: Number of null checks detected in the method body.

assertions.description: Channel 11: Number of assertion statements detected (assert, Preconditions, etc.).

logStatements.description: Channel 12: Number of log statements detected (logger calls, console output, etc.).

validationAnnotations.description: Channel 13: Number of validation annotations detected (@NotNull, @Valid, @Size, class-validator, Joi, Zod, etc.).

oneOf.description: Simple list of injected dependency type names.

dependencies.description: Injected dependencies (field types) used by the method.

contractChecks.description: Contract method checks indicating whether the method implements standard contracts.

intentDensityScore.description: Composite Intent Signal Density (ISD) score from 0.0 (no signals) to 1.0 (maximum density).

isdBreakdown.description: Detailed breakdown of the ISD score showing per-channel contributions and weighted calculation.

NameSemanticsSignal.description: Channel 1: Semantics extracted from the method name by decomposing it into verb, object, and inferred intent category.

verb.description: Primary verb extracted from the method name (e.g. 'find', 'create', 'validate', 'delete').

object.description: Object of the verb extracted from the method name (e.g. 'User', 'Order', 'Configuration').

intent.description: Inferred intent category based on the verb (e.g. 'query', 'mutation', 'validation', 'transformation').

implies.description: Behavioral implications inferred from the name (e.g. ['returns single result', 'may throw NotFoundException']).

GuardClauseSignal.description: A structured guard clause detected at a method entry point, representing a precondition check.

error.description: Error or exception thrown when the guard fails (e.g. 'IllegalArgumentException', 'ValidationError').

boundary.description: Numeric boundary extracted from the guard condition.

BranchSignal.description: Structured analysis of conditional branching within the method.

conditions.description: List of branch condition expressions (e.g. ['status == ACTIVE', 'role == ADMIN']).

paths.description: Descriptions of the distinct execution paths (e.g. ['return cached', 'fetch and cache', 'throw error']).

exhaustive.description: Whether the branching covers all possible cases (e.g. switch with default, if-else chain).

NameBehaviorGapSignal.description: Channel 4: A detected discrepancy between what the method name implies and what the body actually does.

nameImplies.description: What the method name suggests it does (e.g. 'validates input data').

actualBehavior.description: What the method body actually does that differs from the name (e.g. 'also persists to database').

severity.description: Severity of the name-behavior gap: 'error' for serious mismatches, 'warning' for notable discrepancies, 'info' for minor observations.

ReturnTypeSignal.description: Channel 5: Analysis of the method's return type and what it implies about the method's intent.

type.description: The return type as a string (e.g. 'Optional<User>', 'List<String>', 'void', 'CompletableFuture<Result>').

nullable.description: Whether the return type can be null (true for Optional, nullable annotations, etc.).

collection.description: Whether the return type is a collection (List, Set, Map, Stream, array, etc.).

DataFlowSignal.description: Channel 6: Data flow analysis showing what state the method reads from and writes to.

reads.description: Fields, repositories, or external sources read from (e.g. ['userRepository', 'this.cache', 'configService']).

writes.description: Fields, repositories, or external targets written to (e.g. ['auditLog', 'this.lastUpdated', 'eventBus']).

type.description: Classification of the data flow pattern: 'passthrough' passes data through, 'aggregation' combines multiple sources, 'derived' computes new data from existing, 'generated' creates new data.

LoopPropertiesSignal.description: Channel 7: Analysis of loops, iterations, and stream operations within the method.

hasStreams.description: Whether Java streams, LINQ, iterators, or equivalent functional pipelines are used.

hasEnhancedFor.description: Whether enhanced for-each loops (for-in, for-of, range-based for) are used.

over.description: What collection or data source is being iterated over (e.g. 'users', 'orderItems').

operation.description: The primary operation performed in each iteration (e.g. 'transform', 'filter', 'accumulate').

implies.description: What the loop pattern implies about the method's intent (e.g. 'batch processing', 'search', 'aggregation').

ErrorHandlingSignal.description: Channel 8: Analysis of error handling patterns in the method body.

catchBlocks.description: Number of catch/except/recover blocks in the method.

handling.description: The primary error handling strategy: 'wrap_and_throw' re-throws wrapped, 'retry' attempts again, 'log_and_continue' logs and proceeds, 'fallback' uses alternative logic.

ConstantSignal.description: A structured constant reference with its value and implied meaning.

value.description: Constant value as a string representation (e.g. '3', '30000', 'ACTIVE').

type.description: The data type of the constant (e.g. 'int', 'String', 'Duration').

implies.description: What this constant implies about the method's intent (e.g. 'retry logic', 'timeout handling', 'status filtering').

DependencySignal.description: A structured dependency reference indicating an injected service or component.

ContractChecksSignal.description: Flags indicating whether the method implements standard object contracts.

hasEquals.description: Whether the method implements or calls equals().

hasHashCode.description: Whether the method implements or calls hashCode().

hasCompareTo.description: Whether the method implements or calls compareTo().

hasToString.description: Whether the method implements or calls toString().

ISDBreakdown.description: Detailed breakdown of the Intent Signal Density (ISD) score showing individual channel contributions.

channelScores.description: Per-channel scores before weighting, each ranging from 0.0 to 1.0.

naming.description: Score for Channel 1 (nameSemantics) — how well the method name conveys intent.

guardClauses.description: Score for Channel 2 (guardClauses) — presence and quality of precondition checks.

branches.description: Score for Channel 3 (branches) — branching complexity and coverage.

nameBehaviorGap.description: Score for Channel 4 (nameBehaviorGap) — alignment between name and behavior (1.0 = no gap).

returnType.description: Score for Channel 5 (returnType) — how informative the return type is.

dataFlow.description: Score for Channel 6 (dataFlow) — clarity of data read/write patterns.

loopProperties.description: Score for Channel 7 (loopProperties) — iteration pattern documentation.

errorHandling.description: Score for Channel 8 (errorHandling) — error handling strategy clarity.

constants.description: Score for Channel 9 (constants) — meaningful constant usage.

nullChecks.description: Score for Channel 10 (nullChecks) — null safety practices.

assertions.description: Score for Channel 11 (assertions) — presence of assertion statements.

logStatements.description: Score for Channel 12 (logStatements) — observability via logging.

validationAnnotations.description: Score for Channel 13 (validationAnnotations) — declarative validation coverage.

weightedScore.description: Final weighted ISD score computed by applying channel weights (sum of weights = 1.0) to individual channel scores.

CrossChannelVerification.description: Results of verifying consistency across DSTI channels for all methods. Detects contradictions such as a method named 'find' that writes data, or a method with guard clauses but no validation annotations.

issues.description: List of cross-channel consistency issues detected.

methodsChecked.description: Total number of methods analyzed for cross-channel consistency.

issueCount.description: Total number of cross-channel issues found.

CrossChannelIssue.description: A single cross-channel consistency issue where two channels provide contradictory or inconsistent signals.

method.description: Fully qualified name of the method where the issue was detected.

channel1.description: First channel involved in the inconsistency (e.g. 'nameSemantics').

channel2.description: Second channel involved in the inconsistency (e.g. 'dataFlow').

severity.description: Severity of the cross-channel issue: 'error' for direct contradictions, 'warning' for likely inconsistencies, 'info' for observations.

message.description: Human-readable description of the cross-channel inconsistency (e.g. 'Method named findUser writes to auditLog — name implies read-only query').

GapReport.description: Report identifying methods with missing or incomplete intent channel data, providing recommendations for improving documentation coverage.

gaps.description: List of methods with identified intent channel gaps.

recommendations.description: Aggregate recommendations for improving intent coverage across the codebase (e.g. 'Add @DocGuard annotations to 12 methods with implicit preconditions').

totalMethods.description: Total number of methods analyzed in the gap report.

methodsWithGaps.description: Number of methods that have at least one missing channel.

GapEntry.description: A single method's gap analysis showing which intent channels are missing and what to do about it.

method.description: Fully qualified name of the method with gaps.

missingChannels.description: List of channel names that are missing or empty for this method (e.g. ['guardClauses', 'errorHandling', 'dataFlow']).

recommendation.description: Specific recommendation for addressing the gaps in this method (e.g. 'Add guard clauses for the userId parameter and document the error handling strategy').

severity.description: Severity of the gap: 'high' for public API methods missing critical channels, 'medium' for internal methods with notable gaps, 'low' for minor omissions.
